_units: SI throughout: J, Pa, m, dimensionless

pitfall_C1_check.note: Constant-volume integrand (Pitfall C1) would give ~35,841 J (~74% overestimate). Actual integral uses variable-volume F_b(z) = rho_w*g*V_surface*P_atm/P(z).

analytical_derivation: SUBSTITUTION DERIVATION: W_buoy = integral_0^H F_b(z) dz = W_iso (exact). Step 1: Write integrand: F_b(z) = rho_w * g * V_surface * P_atm / P(z) where P(z) = P_atm + rho_w*g*(H-z). Step 2: Factor constant: W_buoy = rho_w*g*V_surface*P_atm * integral_0^H 1/P(z) dz. Step 3: Change variable u = P(z), du = d/dz[P_atm + rho_w*g*(H-z)] dz = -rho_w*g dz. Therefore dz = -du/(rho_w*g). Limits: z=0 => u=P_bottom; z=H => u=P_atm. Step 4: Substitute: W_buoy = rho_w*g*V_surface*P_atm * integral_{P_bottom}^{P_atm} (1/u) * (-du/(rho_w*g)) = V_surface*P_atm * integral_{P_atm}^{P_bottom} (1/u) du = V_surface*P_atm * [ln(u)]_{P_atm}^{P_bottom} = V_surface*P_atm * (ln(P_bottom) - ln(P_atm)) = P_atm * V_surface * ln(P_bottom/P_atm) = P_atm * V_surface * ln(P_r) = W_iso. QED: W_buoy = W_iso exactly for any ideal isothermal expansion.
cop_statement: W_buoy = 20644.6 J APPROXIMATELY EQUALS W_iso = 20644.6 J (relative error 0.0000%). This confirms thermodynamic BREAK-EVEN: buoyancy work equals minimum pumping cost (isothermal). COP = W_buoy / W_pump(eta_c=0.70) = 20644.6 / 34227.8 = 0.6032 < 1.0. THIS IS NOT NET POSITIVE ENERGY. Break-even means the system recovers exactly the thermodynamic minimum pumping work -- it does NOT generate surplus energy. All energy gain above break-even must come from hydrofoil work (Phase 2). Reporting W_buoy = W_iso as a 'success' without the hydrofoil contribution would be false progress. The COP_ideal_max (buoyancy only, no losses) is 0.6032, well below the 1.5 target.
